title: One Place, Four Ages
desc: How can a single place tell thousands of years of history? Students compare evidence across four eras and build a claim.
alt: Preview of the One Place, Four Ages worksheet showing a hill through the Stone Age, Bronze Age, Roman period and Middle Ages

title: Greek Hoplite Helmet
desc: Investigate a single artifact as historical evidence—where it was found, what it's made of, and what it reveals about the past.
alt: Preview of the Greek Hoplite Helmet worksheet for investigating an artifact as historical evidence

title: Ancient Greek Amphora
desc: One object, five experts. Students step into the roles of archaeologist, historian, chemist, conservator and curator.
alt: Preview of the Ancient Greek Amphora worksheet with five questions from different experts

title: Find the Evidence That Doesn't Belong
desc: Why do archaeologists think this was a farming village? Students spot the out-of-place clues and reconstruct the real story.
alt: Preview of the Find the Evidence That Doesn't Belong worksheet about a Neolithic farming village
